provenance.source_corpus: Gauging recovery of zooplankton from historical acid and metal contamination: the influence of temporal changes in restoration targets

source_spans.text: Following manipulations, pH increased to c. 6.15 in Middle, Hannah and Lohi Lakes
Following manipulations, pH increased to c. 6.15 in Middle, Hannah and Lohi Lakes
Total copper and nickel contamination also decreased over time in the lakes
Ice-free mean crustacean richness increased from c. 2 to 4 species from 1973 to 1981 to 6.18 species in Clearwater and Lohi Lakes and c. 9.15 species in Hannah and Middle Lakes in 2007
Lake water acidity decreased as acid deposition declined
Lake water acidity decreased as acid deposition declined
Chloride and sodium increased because of winter road salting
Chloride and sodium increased because of winter road salting
small, planktivorous Yellow Perch Perca flavescens have become very abundant in Sudbury lakes
perhaps contributing to poor daphniid recovery
Zooplankton recovery was also likely to be limited by continued metal contamination
Zooplankton recovery was also likely to be limited by continued metal contamination

nodes.name: pH, cation and dissolved organic carbon levels

source_spans.text: Hannah and Middle Lakes also had higher pH, cation and dissolved organic carbon levels
which almost certainly reduced metal bioavailability and metal toxicity
These differences were attributable to regional shifts in zooplankton communities
Our results show that the choice of reference condition can alter recovery assessments
Our results show that the choice of reference condition can alter recovery assessments
